channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI could affect 40% of all jobs, UN says
item.description: The AI market could grow to as much as $4.8 trillion by 2033, according to a new report from the United Nations Conference on Trade and Development. At the same time, the money is expected to be concentrated among a few players and could increase inequality between countries, CNBC reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIOs to leverage AI for real RoI: Ankit Bose, Nasscom AI
item.description: AI is evolving fast as a mega trend and an ecosystem play given the scale, diversity of varied solutions and the maturity levels of AI across each organisation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UK court reveals Apple's fight against government order for backdoor access to encrypted cloud data
item.description: Apple is fighting a British government order for the iPhone maker to provide backdoor access to a cloud data privacy feature.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Meta fact-checking program to officially end Monday
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, will officially end its fact-checking program Monday, a top company official said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Tech hiring dropped by 29K in March, even as US job growth remained solid
item.description: The tech market lost 29,000 jobs in March, even as the unemployment rate for tech-related jobs slipped 0.1% to 3.1%, according to an analysis of US Labor Department data released today.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A cyber call to arms
item.description: Without a call to arms, the United States is dangerously vulnerable today, and still more tomorrow, to increasingly sophisticated and brazen cyberattacks. The post A cyber call to arms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Nearly half of National Weather Service offices have 20% vacancy rates, and experts say it's a risk
item.description: Data obtained by The Associated Press shows that close to half of the local National Weather Service forecast offices have 20% vacancy rates as severe weather chugs across the nation’s heartland.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How AI is steering the media toward a ‘close enough’ standard
item.description: The nonstop cavalcade of announcements in the AI world has created a kind of reality distortion field. There is so much buzz, and even more money, circulating in the industry that it feels almost sacrilegious to doubt that AI will make good on its promises to change the world.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Trump's tariffs are so bad for Big Tech
item.description: President Trump’s sweeping new slate of tariffs is poised to strike a blow at the tech industry, as massive import taxes on China and Taiwan disrupt trade flows that are central to tech firms’ business models.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro could be the most important AI model so far this year
item.description: Google released its new Gemini 2.5 Pro Experimental AI model late last month, and it’s quickly stacked up top marks on a number of coding, math, and reasoning benchmark tests—making it a contender for the world’s best model right now.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hyperlight Wasm points to the future of serverless
item.description: Back when I first wrote about Hyperlight, Microsoft’s minimal hypervisor, I speculated that it had a possible role as a WebAssembly-based alternative to serverless computing tools like Azure Functions, using Kubernetes as a host.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Splunk launches inventory tool to simplify OpenTelemetry monitoring
item.description: Splunk this week announced a new Service Inventory product that uses OpenTelemetry to offer a comprehensive view into service instrumentation, which the observability company says will solve a critical pain point in modern infrastructure monitoring by providing visibility across cloud and Kubernetes
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Leadership, trust and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: As the military navigates the complexities of the 21st-century battlefield, the integration of artificial intelligence is a leadership imperative. The post Leadership, trust and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Google's new AI may know when your house is on fire
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google’s new AI tech may know when your house will burn down
item.description: FireSat is a satellite project designed to detect and track wildfires early, aiming to detect a fire that's the size of a classroom within 20 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rewriting Social Security will be a train wreck
item.description: Government software development is a curious business. When I was a student at the Naval Postgraduate School, I wrote a paper called “There is a Lot of Money to be Made Writing Bad Software for the Federal Government.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Save loved ones' voicemails with Android
item.description: Preserving voicemails securely on Android: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals easy methods to keep memories alive forever.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
